title: Warm Spiced Saudi Arabian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich, comforting dish featuring lamb stock, fragrant spices, and tender lamb, perfect for a date-night dinner. This recipe is a twist on traditional Saudi Arabian cuisine, using a tandoor-inspired cooking method.

ingredients.name: Tandoor spice blend
ingredients.notes: Available at Middle Eastern markets or online.

instructions: Preheat the tandoor or oven to 3750F (190C).
In a small bowl, mix together the tandoor spice blend, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, ginger paste, and garlic paste.
Rub the spice mixture all over the lamb shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the butter in the tandoor or oven-safe pot over medium heat. Add the lamb shoulder and sear until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the lamb stock, dried dates, pistachios, cardamom powder, cinnamon sticks, and cloves to the pot. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated tandoor or oven. Cook for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the tandoor or oven and stir in the rose water (if using).
Season the tagine with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot over couscous or rice.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving.
